tax: Placed at order rank, Crystalline Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an ordered crystal-lattice body fed by mineral supersaturation, not a bound rubble or sediment mass. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct crystal habit with its own seep chemistry and regrowth mode.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a faceted cluster or blade-sheaf that walks on grown crystal and regrows what it sheds. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a litter of shed crystal or fibre and glassy scratch-grooves on the floor — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Crystalline Elementals occupies vugs, crystal seams, and gypsum-curtain chambers fed by mineral seep. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a supersaturated seep nucleates a self-templating lattice around a pressure node and recur with the seep re-saturates and the lattice re-nucleates, sustaining themselves on silica or sulphate supersaturation in seep water.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members pose as natural crystal and rouse to the stimulus their lattice resonates to — sound or touch. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: an ordered crystal body, slashing and piercing immunity, rapid regrowth, and a specific resonance or dryness failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: directed crystal growth, light or sound effects, and self-sharpening blades. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Crystalline Elementals matters because its galleries are the richest crystal sources in the Throat and the most lethal to work. It lets survey reports sort crystal hazards by habit, so a crew knows whether silence or distance keeps them alive without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Crystalline Elementals is the order-rank grouping of elementals whose bodies are ordered crystal lattices grown from mineral-rich seep within the True Elementals. Its members share a self-templating crystal body that grows, sheds, and regrows faster than bulk rock can be fused, and divide below into family groups by the mineral and its habit — hard ringing quartz or soft cleaving gypsum. The Crystalline order is what the Forgemasters most want to harvest and most often die harvesting.
